steps.title: Track the exponents
steps.content: The outputs are powers of $a$: $a^1$, $a^5$, and $a^9$. Since $a>1$, matching outputs means matching exponents. As $x$ increases by $1$, the exponent increases by $4$.

steps.title: Write the exponent rule
steps.content: A linear exponent rule with rate $4$ has the form $4x+b$. Use the first value, $x=1$ gives exponent $1$: $$4(1)+b=1$$ $$b=-3$$ So the exponent is $4x-3$, and $f(x)=a^{4x-3}$.

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content: If $f(k)=a^{29}$, then the exponent must be $29$: $$4k-3=29$$ $$4k=32$$ $$k=8$$ The correct free-response answer is 8.
